clinical_history: 59 year-old female with a history of hypertension and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is seen for lightheadedness. What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: This ECG shows borderline resting sinus tachycardia (P wave rate is about 100 bpm) with 2:1 AV block with a resulting relatively slow ventricular rate of 50 bpm.  The PR interval of the conducted beats is 170 milliseconds; the QRS duration is normal at 90 milliseconds and the QT interval is physiologic at 440 milliseconds.  There are non-specific ST-T wave changes with an undefined, non-diagnostic precordial transition zone (possibly due to lead placement) and borderline left axis deviation. (V2 gives the appearance of an early transition while subsequent precordial leads show R=S.)  She underwent a cardiac electrophysiology study that showed 2:1 block in the AV node with slight improvement in conduction after 1 mg atropine IV. Due to difficult to control blood pressure and the presence of symptomatic 2:1 block, a permanent, dual-chamber pacemaker was implanted.~~~~
